subject: Maerimydra
fact: Maerimydra is a demon-controlled city characterized by a fiery, ash-filled landscape.
source_quote: The party arrived in the demon-controlled city of Maerimydra, observing its fiery, ash-filled landscape

subject: Maerimydra
fact: The city is situated within a cavern 600 feet tall with smoke rising above 250 feet.
source_quote: a cavern 600 feet tall with smoke above 250 feet

subject: Maerimydra
fact: A western plateau in the city features a six-fingered symbol, which is the symbol of Graz'zt.
source_quote: a western plateau with a six-fingered symbol (Graz'zt's symbol)

subject: Maerimydra
fact: An eastern castle in the city contains two towers.
source_quote: an eastern castle with two towers

subject: Maerimydra
fact: A massive coliseum is located in the southern residential district of the city.
source_quote: a massive coliseum in the southern residential district

subject: Maerimydra inhabitants
fact: The city is inhabited by fire giants, armored ogres, hellhounds, drow elves in chains, and demons.
source_quote: The city was filled with fire giants, armored ogres, hellhounds, drow elves in chains, and demons

subject: iron braziers
fact: Huge blazing fires burn in wide iron braziers throughout the city.
source_quote: Huge blazing fires burned in wide iron braziers

subject: rubble removal
fact: The party removed rubble blocking a tunnel to reveal Maerimydra.
source_quote: The party removed rubble blocking the tunnel, revealing Maerimydra

subject: Daein
fact: Daein attacked Ogre #2, dealing 41 hit points of damage.
source_quote: Daein attacked Ogre #2, dealing 41 hit points of damage

subject: Akritas
fact: Akritas finished off Ogre #2 with her bow and Hunter's Mark, dealing 38 hit points.
source_quote: Akritas finished off Ogre #2 with her bow and Hunter's Mark, dealing 38 hit points

subject: Pipyap
fact: Four ogres were tormenting an imp named Pipyap by swinging it around by its barbed tail.
source_quote: four ogres tormenting a smaller creature, an imp, by swinging it around by its barbed tail

subject: imp rescue negotiation
fact: The imp cried out for help, offering information in exchange for rescue.
source_quote: The imp cried out for help, offering information in exchange for rescue

subject: Owlbear
fact: The Owlbear attacked Ogre #4, dealing 18 hit points.
source_quote: The Owlbear attacked Ogre #4, dealing 18 hit points

subject: Daein
fact: Ogre #4 charged Daein, hitting for 13 points of damage, which Daein reduced to 6 using Protective Field.
source_quote: Ogre #4 charged Daein, hitting for 13 points of damage, which Daein reduced to 6 using Protective Field

subject: Felkur
fact: Felkur cast Fireball, hitting Ogres #1, #3, and #4 for 28 damage each.
source_quote: Felkur cast Fireball, hitting Ogres #1, #3, and #4 for 28 damage each

subject: Felkur
fact: Felkur used another spell to hit Ogre #3 for 16 damage, though the ogre made its save for half damage, taking 8.
source_quote: used another spell to hit Ogre #3 for 16 damage (which made its save for half damage, taking 8)

subject: Bramgrim
fact: Bramgrim attempted to negotiate with the imp during combat.
source_quote: Bramgrim attempted to negotiate with the imp during combat

subject: Daein
fact: Daein finished off Ogre #4 with a critical hit.
source_quote: Daein finished off Ogre #4 with a critical hit

subject: Akritas
fact: Akritas killed Ogre #3 with her bow and Hunter's Mark, dealing 38 hit points.
source_quote: Akritas killed Ogre #3 with her bow and Hunter's Mark, dealing 38 hit points

subject: Owlbear
fact: The Owlbear attacked Ogre #1, dealing 35 hit points.
source_quote: The Owlbear attacked Ogre #1, dealing 35 hit points

subject: Felkur
fact: Felkur used a firebolt to finish off the last remaining Ogre #1.
source_quote: Felkur used a firebolt to finish off the last remaining Ogre #1

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap claimed to know everything about the city and offered his services in exchange for rescue.
source_quote: Pipyap, offered his services in exchange for rescue, claiming to know everything about the city

subject: contract negotiation
fact: The party rejected Pipyap's initial standard contract and negotiated a new one.
source_quote: The party rejected the initial contract and negotiated a new one

subject: Felkur
fact: Felkur and Bramgrim successfully crafted a favorable contract with Pipyap, leveraging their past experience with an Ultraloth.
source_quote: Felkur and Bramgrim successfully crafted a favorable contract with Pipyap, leveraging their past experience with an Ultraloth

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap revealed he was brought to the prime material plane by a magic user's mistake and enjoys being there.
source_quote: Pipyap revealed he was brought to the prime material plane by a magic user's mistake and enjoys being there

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap has no desire to return to the Nine Hells.
source_quote: having no desire to return to the Nine Hells

subject: Pipyap
fact: Pipyap explained that his previous contracts had loopholes that allowed him to remain free on the prime material plane.
source_quote: Pipyap explained his previous contracts had loopholes that allowed him to remain free on the prime material plane

subject: Pipyap
fact: The party fed Pipyap, who complained about the lack of salt in their food.
source_quote: The party fed Pipyap, who complained about the lack of salt in their food

subject: Hledh
fact: Hledh is a huge fire giant queen who rules Maerimydra and lives in the eastern part of the city.
source_quote: the ruler of Maerimydra is Hledh, a huge fire giant queen who lives in the eastern part of the city

subject: Maerimydra social hierarchy
fact: The social hierarchy in Maerimydra consists of demons, giants, ogres, rothi, devils, and slaves.
source_quote: Pipyap explained the social hierarchy: demons, giants, ogres, rothi, devils, and slaves

subject: Veperyd
fact: Veperyd is a fiendish giant in charge of the Coliseum.
source_quote: one in charge of the Coliseum (Veperyd)

subject: unnamed fiendish giant
fact: Another fiendish giant is managing the six-fingered courtyard.
source_quote: another managing the six-fingered courtyard

subject: Mulmaster wizard
fact: A wizard from Mulmaster has shown up for dinner at Vraxen's house.
source_quote: a wizard from Mulmaster has shown up for dinner at Vraxen's house

subject: Vraxen
fact: Vraxen lives in the Spire of Inevitable Reckoning.
source_quote: Vraxen lives in the Spire of Inevitable Reckoning

subject: Spire of Inevitable Reckoning
fact: The Spire of Inevitable Reckoning contains the teleportation circle and the book the party seeks.
source_quote: which also contains the teleportation circle and the book the party seeks

subject: distraction plan
fact: Pipyap suggested a plan to cause a distraction by instigating a fight between Vraxen and Veperyd over Veperyd's theft of betting money.
source_quote: instigating a fight between Vraxen and Veperyd over Veperyd's theft of betting money

subject: party goals
fact: The party's goals are to acquire a demonically infused giant and secure a specific book.
source_quote: The party discussed their two main goals: acquiring a demonically infused giant and securing a specific book

subject: party strategy
fact: The party decided to prioritize getting the fiendish giant first, then provoking the war, then stealing the book.
source_quote: They decided to prioritize getting the fiendish giant first, then provoking the war, then stealing the book

subject: Bramgrim
fact: Bramgrim recalled that Hillsfar was controlled by Graz'zt, suggesting a bluff to enter the dinner as merchants from Hillsfar or as staff.
source_quote: Bramgrim recalled that Hillsfar was controlled by Graz'zt, suggesting a bluff to enter the dinner as merchants from Hillsfar or as staff

subject: healing
fact: The party decided to use second wind to heal Daein and Felkur to full health before proceeding.
source_quote: The party decided to use second wind to heal Daein and Felkur to full health before proceeding

subject: Black Ember Outpost
fact: The Black Ember Outpost is a fortress built into a cave wall in the Underdark, made of polished obsidian.
source_quote: A fortress built into a cave wall in the Underdark, made of polished obsidian

subject: Black Ember Outpost
fact: The Black Ember Outpost served as a base for fire giants, ogres, and hellhounds.
source_quote: It served as a base for fire giants, ogres, and hellhounds

subject: Black Ember Outpost
fact: The party previously cleared out the Black Ember Outpost during their assault.
source_quote: The party previously cleared it out during their assault

subject: Storeroom (Black Ember Outpost)
fact: The storeroom within the Black Ember Outpost contained a hidden chest with treasures.
source_quote: It contained a hidden chest with treasures

subject: Storeroom (Black Ember Outpost)
fact: The storeroom served as the entry point to the Path of Shadows.
source_quote: served as the entry point to the Path of Shadows

subject: Well (Black Ember Outpost)
fact: The well in the storeroom of the Black Ember Outpost is recognized as the entrance to the Path of Shadows.
source_quote: recognized as the entrance to the Path of Shadows

subject: descent to Maerimydra
fact: The party descended into the well to reach Maerimydra.
source_quote: The party descended into it to reach Maerimydra
